chore: update dependabot config to ignore @types/node major versions (#1295)

`@types/node` major versions track Node.js major versions. Allowing
dependabot to bump `@types/node` across majors introduces type
definitions for a runtime the project doesn't develop against — silently
expanding the apparent API surface and risking broken builds when Node
removes or renames APIs.

## Changes

- **`.github/dependabot.yml`**: Added an `ignore` rule for `@types/node`
scoped to `version-update:semver-major` in the `npm` ecosystem block.
Minor and patch updates continue to flow through normally.


No QA Required

---------

commit.message:
feat: harden project memory with injection scanning, atomic writes, budget limits, and staleness tracking (#95)

- Add scanMemoryContent() to reject prompt-injection payloads, exfil patterns,
  and invisible Unicode in candidate/summary/architecture text
- Sanitize architecture snapshots on read, write, and bootstrap paths so no
  unsanitized text reaches the agent system prompt
- Switch project-memory JSON/markdown and transcript-index writes to atomic
  temp-file + rename with retry and fallback on Windows EPERM
- Add configurable byte budget for stored candidates with confidence/recency/
  reinforcement-aware pruning when the limit is exceeded
- Track lastReinforcedAt per candidate; mark unreinforced candidates stale
  after a configurable number of later sessions
- Repair malformed JSONL trailing lines in both append() and readEvents() so
  post-crash events are never lost
- Preserve the persisted active marker during conflict normalization so a
  low-confidence correction is not overridden by a higher-confidence
  superseded entry on reload

Co-authored-by: heping duan <hduan10@ford.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add MCP schema-patching proxy for TensorZero

TensorZero 2026.4.0 MCP server emits a `create_datapoints` tool whose
`output` property schema is missing a `"type"` key, causing OpenAI to
reject the whole tool list at function-registration time.

Add a lightweight aiohttp proxy (infrastructure/tensorzero/mcp-proxy/)
that intercepts `tools/list` SSE responses and recursively injects
`"type": "object"` on any property schema node that lacks it.  The
proxy runs on port 3001 → TensorZero MCP port 3000 as a Docker Compose
service and restarts automatically.

Remove TensorZero from local .mcp.json (clients should use port 3001
via the proxy; direct port 3000 still works for swarm REST API).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add 17 new health checks (LOG, BAK, DB options, STOR, IDX-011, MAINT-002, CFG-006)

New health checks added:
- LOG-001: High VLF count (>200 = Medium, >1000 = High)
- LOG-002: Log reuse wait blocked (contextual guidance per wait type)
- BAK-001: Full backup recency (overdue/never)
- BAK-002: Log backups for FULL/BULK recovery model
- BAK-003: No recent differential backup gap
- DB-001: AUTO_SHRINK enabled (High)
- DB-002: AUTO_CLOSE enabled (Medium)
- DB-003: PAGE_VERIFY not CHECKSUM (High)
- DB-004: RCSI disabled with active blocking (Info advisory)
- DB-005: Query Store disabled (Low)
- DB-006: Query Store READ_ONLY/full (Medium)
- STOR-001: Low disk space on database volume
- STOR-002: Data and log files on same volume
- IDX-011: Columnstore index opportunity (Deep only)
- MAINT-002: SQL Agent job failures in last 7 days
- CFG-006: Known-harmful global trace flags (1117, 1118, 3625, 8744, 9481, 4199)

New data collection:
- DatabaseOptionsInfo from sys.databases (AUTO_SHRINK, AUTO_CLOSE, PAGE_VERIFY, RCSI, QS)
- VolumeInfo from sys.dm_os_volume_stats per database file
- FailedAgentJobInfo from msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory (permission-guarded)
- GlobalTraceFlagInfo from DBCC TRACESTATUS(-1)

New check files: LogHealthChecks.cs, BackupPolicyChecks.cs, DatabaseOptionChecks.cs,
StorageChecks.cs, MaintenanceChecks.cs

Schema version bumped to 1.5. Quick: 28->43, Deep: 36->52.
New report sections: Database Options, Storage Volumes, SQL Agent Job Failures, Global Trace Flags.
Tests: 82 -> 99 (17 new tests).

fix: thread safety, NVS wear, blocking delay, devirtualization

Song title race: Replace single song_title_ buffer with double-buffer
pattern (title_bufs_[2]). Core 0 audio callbacks write to the inactive
buffer then atomically flip title_read_idx_. Core 1 always reads from
the stable buffer, eliminating torn reads without a mutex.

Station ordering race: Copy station URL into pending_url_ buffer before
setting pending_connect_ flag. Core 0 audio task reads pending_url_
directly instead of dereferencing stations_[current_station_], which
could read a stale index if Core 1 changed it between flag set and
consumption.

Non-blocking PA enable: Replace 200ms delay() in connect_station_()
with a deferred state machine. pa_pending_ flag and pa_pending_ms_
timestamp are set on station connect; loop() enables the PA pin after
200ms without blocking the ESPHome main loop.

Debounced NVS saves: Volume changes from HA (set/up/down), touch
slider, and touch buttons all use mark_vol_dirty_() instead of
immediate NVS writes. flush_vol_if_dirty_() in loop() commits to
flash only after 3s of inactivity, preventing flash wear from rapid
volume adjustments. Also fixes set_volume_direct() (touch slider)
which previously never persisted volume to NVS.

Devirtualization: Mark InternetRadio, I2SBridge, WinampDisplay as
final classes, enabling compiler to eliminate vtable lookups for
loop(), setup(), get_setup_priority() per ESPHome 2026.3.0 guidance.

commit.message:
Fix closure cell leaks, for-let force_local_let restore, and ClearLocalCells opcode

Three interrelated fixes for block-scoped closure/TDZ correctness:

1. Save/restore block_stmt_depth and force_local_let in all 6 function
   compilation paths (FnDecl, arrow, fn expr, async gen, gen, class ctor)
   so these states don't leak from enclosing blocks into nested functions.

2. Add ClearLocalCells opcode (110) to clean up stale closure cells between
   loop iterations and at block scope exits. Emitted in for-of, for-in,
   destructuring loops, and block statements. Prevents cross-iteration cell
   leaking at the top level.

3. Fix for-statement init clause to save/restore force_local_let instead of
   unconditionally resetting it to false. A for(let i...) inside a bare
   block (which sets force_local_let=true) was destroying the outer block's
   state, causing subsequent class declarations to emit DefineGlobal instead
   of SetLocal — leaving their pre-declared TDZ cells uninitialized.

4. Remove is_module_mode guard on MakeClosure top-level cell persistence
   so script-mode closures also get live bindings (not snapshots).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: BT button touch zone + BBC sample rate on BT speaker

Touch offset: Remove +12 Y offset from touch coordinates. Raw GT911
register reads already return screen-space coordinates (LovyanGFX
configured GT911 rotation during init). The +12 was calibrated for
LovyanGFX getTouch() output in the original firmware, causing a 12px
upward shift of all touch zones when reading raw registers.

BT button zone: Expand touch area from exact 16px to 30px (±6/+24)
since the button sits near the screen bottom edge where users
naturally touch slightly low.

BBC audio: Parse 'SampleRate (Hz):' from audio info callback and
update I2SBridge::bridge_sample_rate. BBC streams at 48kHz but the
bridge was stuck at 44100Hz default, causing audio to play at 91.9%
speed ('funny voice' distortion). The resampler in audio_process_i2s
now correctly engages for non-44100Hz streams.

feat: Instant tip pre-fetch with transcript invalidation (#47)

* feat: instant tip pre-fetch with transcript invalidation

Pre-generate coaching tips in background so button click serves instantly (0ms).

Background changes (src/background/index.ts):
- 5-second prefetch loop generates tips silently during active calls
- Transcript invalidation: cached tip discarded on every new FINAL transcript,
  fresh tip prefetched within 500ms
- REQUEST_NEXT_TIP serves cached tip instantly, triggers next prefetch
- Falls back to live Lambda request if no cached tip available

Lambda quick wins:
- max_tokens 250 -> 150 (tips are ~50-80 tokens, saves ~200ms)
- Context window 25 -> 15 transcripts (saves ~200ms)
- Context chars 2400 -> 1800

Cost impact: ~3.5x vs manual-only (~\.55/hr vs \.16/hr per agent).
With prompt caching: ~\.18/hr per agent.

5/5 test scenarios pass against live AWS (3 skipped due to 429 rate limits).

* fix: discard stale prefetch responses after transcript invalidation

Race condition: when a new transcript arrived while a prefetch was in-flight,
the stale response would be stored with a fresh timestamp. User would then
get an outdated tip on button press.

Fix: needsFreshPrefetch flag marks in-flight responses as stale. When the
stale response arrives, it's discarded and a fresh prefetch triggers
immediately with the latest conversation context.

* fix: allow concurrent prefetch to eliminate 5s serialization delay

Previous fix serialized requests: wait for stale response (~2s), discard,
then start fresh (~2s) = ~4-5s gap with no cached tip.

New approach: reset isPrefetchInFlight on invalidation so fresh prefetch
fires immediately (500ms debounce). Stale response arrives and is briefly
cached, then overwritten by fresh response ~1s later. Tip available in
~2.5s instead of ~5s.

* feat: streaming AI tips on button click

Replace prefetch system with streaming: button click triggers Lambda which
streams Anthropic response chunks through WebSocket in real-time.

Lambda changes:
- generateAITipStreaming() uses anthropic.messages.stream()
- Each text chunk pushed via sendToConnection(TIP_CHUNK)
- Final INTELLIGENCE_UPDATE returned as before (backward compat)

Extension changes:
- Removed all prefetch state, timers, invalidation, cache logic
- Auto-analysis restored to 10s interval, intelligence only (skipTip=true)
- TIP_CHUNK handler forwards streaming chunks to UI
- SidePanel renders text progressively as chunks arrive
- Zustand store tracks streamingTip state

Benefits:
- First words appear in ~300-500ms (vs ~2.5s wait before)
- Always fresh (uses latest transcripts, no staleness)
- 70% cheaper (~\.16/hr vs \.55/hr per agent)
- Simpler code (removed ~80 lines of prefetch complexity)

* fix: use for-await pattern for Anthropic streaming

stream.on('text', async ...) does not await async callbacks, causing
sendToConnection() calls to fire-and-forget. Switch to for-await over
stream events to properly await each chunk delivery.

Tested: 7 TIP_CHUNK messages received, first chunk at 2.5s, full tip
streamed and finalized with INTELLIGENCE_UPDATE.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add loading indicator for tip generation

- Set isGeneratingTip=true immediately on button click (shows skeleton)
- Show bouncing dots during both loading and streaming phases
- Add blinking cursor at end of streaming text
- Disable button and show 'Generating...' while streaming
- Prevent double-clicks during generation
- Clear loading state on first chunk arrival or error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: reset generation state between calls + error handling

- Reset isGeneratingTip and streamingTip in CLEAR_SESSION handler
- Handle background error response (no conversation, WS disconnected)
- Add 15s safety timeout to prevent stuck loading state
- Prevents button from staying disabled after call transitions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* debug: add TIP_CHUNK logging to diagnose streaming UI issue

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: persist coachingPending to survive service worker restarts

Root cause: coachingPending was only in-memory. When Chrome kills
the MV3 service worker between calls (~30s idle), it resets to false.
Next call's CALL_STARTED handler skips startCaptureAndCoaching(),
so no WebSocket connection is established and tips fail silently.

Fix:
- Persist coachingPending to chrome.storage.local via updateExtensionState
- Restore coachingPending (+ user session) at module load time
- Add storage fallback check in both CALL_STARTED handlers
- Fix error response wrapping: store now checks inner result.success
- broadcastToUI now logs errors instead of silently swallowing them

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: skip DB read on tip requests by passing transcripts from client

Extension sends last 20 transcripts with speaker labels in the
getIntelligence WebSocket message. Lambda uses client-provided
transcripts when cached intelligence exists (FAST PATH), skipping
the Postgres round-trip (~200-400ms savings per tip click).

Auto-analysis loop (10s) still reads from DB for full intelligence.
DB writes unchanged — transcripts still saved for history/analytics.

Changes:
- Store: send rich transcripts array (speaker + text) with REQUEST_NEXT_TIP
- Background: forward transcripts to WebSocket service
- WebSocket service: accept optional transcripts param in getIntelligence
- Lambda: use client transcripts for tip path, handle chronological vs DESC ordering

When customer says 'another time' or 'I'm busy right now' without

When customer finishes speaking, auto-generate a tip in the background

- INSTANT PATH: cached tip served immediately from memory

* revert: remove speculative pre-gen, warm cache immediately

Speculative pre-generation caused stale/wrong tips when customer

Instead, fire first auto-analysis immediately when coaching starts

on first button click).
